title: Open Policy Agent Resources
description: Schema for Open Policy Agent (OPA) core resources including policy modules, data documents, evaluation inputs and results, query requests, partial evaluation compile requests, and OPA status and configuration. OPA uses the Rego policy language to evaluate authorization and policy decisions against structured input data.

PolicyModule.title: PolicyModule
PolicyModule.description: A Rego policy module stored in OPA. Policy modules define rules, functions, and sets that OPA evaluates when making policy decisions. Each module has a unique ID and contains Rego source code that is parsed into an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) for evaluation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the policy module within the OPA instance. Used in API paths to reference the specific policy. Typically uses a path-like format (e.g., 'authz/main').

raw.description: Raw Rego source code for the policy module. Contains package declarations, import statements, rule definitions, and function definitions written in the Rego policy language.

ast.description: Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) representation of the parsed Rego policy module. Returned by OPA when the policy is retrieved and contains the structured representation of all rules and expressions.

PolicyAST.title: PolicyAST
PolicyAST.description: The Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) representation of a parsed Rego policy module. The AST is produced by OPA's parser and reflects the complete structure of the policy including all packages, imports, rules, and expressions.

package.description: The package declaration identifying the namespace this policy belongs to

imports.description: Import statements that bring external packages or data into scope

rules.description: Rule definitions within the policy module, including complete rules, partial rules, and functions

PolicyRule.title: PolicyRule
PolicyRule.description: A single rule definition within an OPA policy module. Rules can be complete rules (with a single value), partial rules (contributing to a set or object), or functions (with parameters). Rules are evaluated by OPA when policy decisions are requested.

default.description: When true, this rule provides the default value for the rule head when no other rule body evaluates to true

head.description: The rule head defining the rule's name and value

value.description: The value the rule evaluates to

body.description: The rule body containing the conditions (expressions) that must be satisfied for the rule to be true

items.description: An expression in the rule body

DataDocument.title: DataDocument
DataDocument.description: A data document stored in OPA's virtual document model. Data documents are structured JSON values stored at hierarchical paths under the 'data' root. They can be static base documents loaded at startup, or virtual documents computed by policy rules.

result.description: The value of the document at the requested path. Can be any JSON type: object, array, string, number, boolean, or null.

metrics.description: Performance metrics for the document evaluation, included when metrics=true query parameter is set

decision_id.description: Unique identifier for this policy decision, included when the decision_logs plugin is enabled. Used to correlate API responses with decision log entries.

provenance.description: Build and version information about the OPA instance, included when provenance=true query parameter is set

explanation.description: Step-by-step trace of rule evaluation, included when the explain query parameter is set

PolicyDecisionRequest.title: PolicyDecisionRequest
PolicyDecisionRequest.description: Request body for a policy decision evaluation sent to the OPA Data API. The input document is passed to OPA's policy rules as the 'input' global variable and can contain any JSON-structured data such as HTTP request attributes, user identity, or resource properties.

input.description: The input document made available to policy rules as the 'input' variable. Can be any JSON type, but is typically an object containing context about the action being authorized.

QueryRequest.title: QueryRequest
QueryRequest.description: Request body for executing an ad-hoc Rego query against OPA. Queries are evaluated in the context of the loaded policy and data documents. Ad-hoc queries are useful for testing and debugging but production use should prefer the Data API.

query.description: Rego query expression to evaluate (e.g., 'data.authz.allow == true')

input.description: Input document to make available as the 'input' variable during query evaluation

imports.description: Additional import statements to include in the query evaluation context

package.description: Package namespace for resolving relative rule references in the query

unknowns.description: Variables treated as unknown during partial evaluation for the Compile API

items.description: A reference to treat as unknown (e.g., 'input.user')

QueryResult.title: QueryResult
QueryResult.description: Result of a Rego query evaluation. The result set contains one binding for each way the query can be satisfied. An empty result set means the query is undefined (not satisfied).

result.description: Set of bindings that satisfy the query. Each entry maps variable names to their bound values.

items.description: A binding set satisfying the query

bindings.description: Map of variable names to their bound values in this result

expressions.description: Expression results in the same order as the query expressions

value.description: The value the expression evaluated to

decision_id.description: Unique decision identifier for correlation with decision logs

CompileRequest.title: CompileRequest
CompileRequest.description: Request body for the OPA Compile API which performs partial evaluation of a Rego query. Partial evaluation simplifies the policy by evaluating known inputs and leaving unknowns as variables, producing a simplified set of conditions. The result can be used to push policy enforcement into external systems.

query.description: Rego query to partially evaluate (e.g., 'data.authz.allow == true')

input.description: Partial input document with known values. Unknown portions should be omitted.

unknowns.description: References to treat as unknown during partial evaluation. These become variables in the residual output. Defaults to ['input'] if not specified.

items.description: A Rego reference to treat as unknown (e.g., 'input.user.role')

options.description: Compiler options controlling partial evaluation behavior

disableInlining.description: List of rule references to exclude from inlining during partial evaluation

CompileResult.title: CompileResult
CompileResult.description: Result of partial evaluation from the OPA Compile API. The result is a set of support rules and a partial set of queries representing the conditions that remain after known inputs are evaluated.

result.description: The partial evaluation result containing residual queries and support rules

queries.description: Residual queries after partial evaluation. Each query is an array of expressions that must be true for the policy to allow the request.

support.description: Support rules generated during partial evaluation that may be needed to evaluate the residual queries

EvaluationMetrics.title: EvaluationMetrics
EvaluationMetrics.description: Performance metrics collected during OPA policy evaluation. Returned when the metrics=true query parameter is included in API requests.

timer_rego_query_eval_ns.description: Time spent evaluating the Rego query in nanoseconds

timer_rego_query_parse_ns.description: Time spent parsing the query in nanoseconds

timer_rego_query_compile_ns.description: Time spent compiling the query in nanoseconds

timer_rego_module_parse_ns.description: Time spent parsing policy modules in nanoseconds

timer_rego_module_compile_ns.description: Time spent compiling policy modules in nanoseconds

Provenance.title: Provenance
Provenance.description: Build and version information about the OPA instance that processed the request. Useful for debugging and auditing policy decisions.

build_commit.description: Git commit hash of the OPA build

build_timestamp.description: Timestamp when this OPA binary was built

build_hostname.description: Hostname of the machine that built this OPA binary

bundles.description: Map of bundle names to their current revision/hash information

revision.description: Current revision identifier for the bundle

OPAError.title: OPAError
OPAError.description: Error response returned by the OPA REST API when a request fails. Error responses include a machine-readable code and a human-readable message.

code.description: Machine-readable error code identifying the error category

errors.description: Detailed list of specific errors, particularly for policy compilation errors

code.description: Error code for this specific error

message.description: Error message for this specific error

SourceLocation.title: SourceLocation
SourceLocation.description: Source code location within a Rego policy file, used in error messages and AST nodes to identify where an expression or error occurs.

file.description: Policy module ID or filename containing the source

row.description: Line number in the source file (1-indexed)

col.description: Column number in the source line (1-indexed)

OPAStatus.title: OPAStatus
OPAStatus.description: Status information about the OPA instance including bundle loading state, plugin health, and policy activation status. Returned by the Status API.

bundles.description: Map of bundle names to their current loading status

plugins.description: Map of plugin names to their current state

BundleStatus.title: BundleStatus
BundleStatus.description: Loading and activation status for an OPA bundle. Bundles contain policy modules and/or data documents loaded from remote sources.

name.description: Name of the bundle as configured in OPA

active_revision.description: Currently active revision identifier for this bundle

last_successful_activation.description: Timestamp of the last successful bundle activation

last_successful_download.description: Timestamp of the last successful bundle download

last_successful_request.description: Timestamp of the last successful request to the bundle server

code.description: Error code if the bundle is in an error state

message.description: Error message if the bundle failed to load
